name: Green Pasta with Tomato Sauce and Basil
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Flour (70% semolina, 30% all-purpose, rice, or buckwheat)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the flour in a bowl or on a work surface. Add egg, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
2. Gradually add olive oil and basil puree while mixing until a dough forms.
3. Turn the dough onto a floured surface and knead until smooth.
4. Dust with flour, roll out using a pasta machine, and cut into noodles. Do not dry the noodles before cooking.
1. Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add mirepoix, garlic puree, bay leaves, thyme, oregano, and peppercorns. Cook for 5 minutes to release flavors.
3. Add white wine and cook briefly.
4. Stir in basil, chopped tomatoes, tomato puree, additional garlic puree, chicken stock, and veal stock. Simmer for 15 minutes until vegetables are tender.
5. Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into another saucepan.
6. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
7. Reduce heat and simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
1. Cook pasta in large pot of boiling water until al dente, then drain.
2. Heat olive oil, basil puree, salt, and pepper in a sauté pan over medium heat.
3. Add cooked pasta and toss to coat with the sauce.
4. Serve hot, garnished with chopped basil leaves and chives.
